properties.remark: SFWMD Lake Okeechobee West End reported a wind gust of 40 mph.

Updates LSR to Tornado event type after survey determined EF0 tornado occurred. Playground equipment knocked down, chairs blown over, small trees blown over/uprooted.

Several pictures showing uprooted trees, torn tree branches, pieces of a broken fence, and tilted/leaning chain-link fences. Reports coincide with a supercell that moved across the area around that time. Possible tornado.

Updates LSR to Tornado event type after survey determined EF0 tornado occurred. Several pictures showing uprooted trees, torn tree branches, pieces of a broken fence, and tilted/leaning chain-link fences. Reports coincide with a supercell that moved across the area around that time. Possible tornado.
Playground equipment knocked down, chairs blown over, small trees blown over/uprooted.

Social media photo of a large tree down on the road in the High Point community of Delray Beach. Time estimated via KAMX reflectivity.

Off duty NWS employee reports several large tree branches down near the intersection of NW 99th Ave and Johnson Street as a severe thunderstorm downburst occurred. Time estimated via KAMX radar.
Off duty NWS employee reported quarter size hail in Pembroke Pines.
FLL/HWO Emergency Manager provided a second update that multiple aircraft at North Perry Airport were damaged beyond repair. No injuries were reported.

Preliminary report from the FLL/HWO Emergency Manager stating that a Cessna was lifted up and onto a fuel truck during a severe thunderstorm downburst. Time estimation of 5:44pm is based on KAMX radar data and the North Perry ASOS recording 53 mph. An airport field team is in transit to access additional damage to the facility, this report will be updated as needed.

Miramar Police are on the scene in the 2700 block of Sunshine Boulevard due to downed power lines and storm related damage due to a severe thunderstorm downburst. Sunshine Blvd is currently closed at the 2600 block as FPL and Public Works work on clearing the street. Time of the report is estimated via KAMX radar.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 2 NNW Coral Gables. Reports of downed power lines, tree limbs and side fencing associated with a strong thunderstorm that moved across the area. Timing is estimated based on KAMX reflectivity.
Reports of downed power lines and fencing associated with a strong thunderstorm that moved across the area. Timing is estimated based on KAMX reflectivity.
ASOS station KHWO Pembroke Pines reported a wind gust of 52 KTS/60 MPH associated with a strong thunderstorm. Station elevation is 7 ft.

Miramar Police reports a fallen tree is obstructing a lane at the intersection of Pembroke Road and 62nd Ave, in relation to a strong thunderstorm that moved over the area earlier this afternoon. Time estimated from radar data.
Broward County Sheriffs Office reports all lanes of SR-848 at SW 35th Ave are closed due to downed powerline in Hollywood in relation to a strong thunderstorm that moved over the area.

Broken tree limbs and branches at a home in The Hammocks near SW 147 Avenue and N. Kendall Drive. Time is estimated based on radar indicated wind gusts near the surface in the 40-50 mph range south of this area in association with a thunderstorm downburst.

A member of the public reported a waterspout over the waters just off the coast of Sunny Isles Beach on Monday afternoon.
Significant flash flooding has been reported on Federal Highway in Boca Raton. 5 to 7 inches of rainfall has occurred in the past 2 hours.
Social media video depicts cars submerged to the top of wheels at A1A and Palmetto Park Road in Boca Raton due to flash flooding.
Social media depicts several stalled cars along NW 4th Street in Deerfield Beach. Water level is about halfway up SUV size tires.
Social media image depicts significant standing water at intersection of SW 18 St. and SW 8th Ave. in Boca Raton. Water covered road and front yards completely and almost entered homes.
Social media video depicts multiple feet of water inside the lobby of the Boca Raton resort associated with heavy rainfall.
Social media depicts several stalled cars in flooded intersection of US 1 and NE 26th Street.
Flash flooding reported on Sunrise Blvd and NE 3rd in Fort Lauderdale.
Portions of West Cypress Creek road is impassable due to flash flooding. Numerous other roads in the area also experiencing flooding.
